title: Retention and compression interaction
description: Retention drops whole cold chunks while compression makes cold chunks columnar and largely immutable; ordering matters.
body: Use when configuring data lifecycle. Retention policies drop entire chunks older than a threshold; compression converts cold chunks to a columnar form that is cheap to scan but expensive to modify. Do not write or backfill into compressed/retained windows — writes may fail or force decompression. Actions: order policies so compression precedes retention age and both sit beyond the active write/lateness window. Validation signals include stable storage and successful cold-range queries. Risk boundary: misordered policies cause either data loss or failed late writes — verify the age thresholds against the lateness bound.
